Bus Timings

Abu Dhabi Bus 916 Timings from Al Mottaqein Mosque to Co-op

May 19, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

Bus 916 links up Al Mottaqein Mosque with Co op, giving an effective means of travel around Al Ain. It has to pass by key landmarks making it frequented by both visitors and daily commuters. Bus 916 is a comfortable and efficient way to travel to work, to explore or to navigate the city. In this guide, we will be looking at the route’s operational details, major stops and what you can see around. We’re here to help you make your way easier and more enjoyable.

Contents

Operational Details

Bus 916 has a regular schedule and regular departures all day. Below is the timetable for the route from both Al Mottaqein Mosque and Co-op:

916 Weekday Morning Schedule

916 Weekday Evening Schedule

916 Weekend Morning Schedule

916 Weekend Evening ScheduleYou can see the full schedule on the official AD Mobility website or click here for Bus 916 timings.

Ticket Pricing: Bus 916 single-zone travel starts at AED 2. The Hafilat Card is the preferred payment method, which can be easily recharged online or at kiosks all around the city.

Route Overview

With Bus 916 you can pass a great deal of significant landmarks and destinations in Al Ain. Key stops along the route include:

1.

  1. Zakhir Health Center: A vital healthcare facility.

  2. Bin Amer Mosque: A place of worship for locals.

4.

  1. Al Ain Sports and Cultural Club: A hub for sports enthusiasts.

  2. Al Zaydiya School: Serving the local educational community.

  3. Zakhir East Police Station: Providing security services to the area.

8.

  1. Emirates Cement: A key industrial landmark.

  2. ADNOC: A stop near the famous oil company facilities.

  3. Emirates for the Manufacture of Metal Structures: A landmark in Al Ain’s industrial sector.

  4. Al Otaibah Mosque: A significant religious site.

  5. Ali Bin Harmal Mosque: Another important mosque in the city.

  6. Al Fattim Show Room: A retail and shopping location.

  7. Al Ashr Street: A busy area with shops and services.

  8. Etisalat: The telecommunications provider’s local stop.

  9. Al Ain Palace Museum: A cultural site offering insights into the city’s royal history.

  10. Post Office: A key point for postal services.

  11. Department of Public Health: Essential for health-related services.

  12. Shiekha Salama Mosque: A local mosque worth visiting.

  13. Al Noor Hospital South: A prominent medical facility.

  14. City Centre: A lively shopping and dining destination.

  15. Mediclinic: A well-known healthcare provider.

  16. Union National Bank: A major banking institution.

25.

  1. Imam Shafi’i Mosque: A historically significant mosque.
Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

  1. Al Ain Hospital: A major healthcare center.

  2. Al Ain Dental Clinic: A specialized dental clinic.

  3. Al Ain Courts Complex: A key part of the city’s legal infrastructure.

  4. Al Ain Municipality West: The local government office.

  5. Al Ain Municipality: Serving the city’s public services.

  6. Department of Immigration and Passports: A crucial administrative location.

  7. Al Jimi Mall: A bustling shopping center.

  8. Zayed The First Street: A main road connecting important areas.

  9. Hazza Bin Zayed Streetadium: A major sports and event venue.

  10. Towayyah Park: A tranquil park for relaxation.

  11. Omar Bin Abdul Aziz Street: A busy street with local shops.

  12. Riyah Al Shamal Street: A notable city road.

  13. Mubarak Mohammad Al Ameri Mosque: A mosque of cultural importance.

  14. Magdi Gharib Al Ahbabi Mosque: A place of worship in the area.

  15. Al Thiniaya Street: A significant street in the city.

  16. Al Bateen Petrol Station: A convenient fuel stop.

  17. Souq Extra: A shopping destination for various goods.

  18. Al Dahma School: A local educational institution.

  19. Salama Bint Butti School: A nearby school for the community.

  20. Omar Ibn Al-Khattab Mosque: A well-known mosque.

  21. Al Salama Street: A main road with shops and amenities.

  22. Co-op: The final stop, a well-known supermarket.

Nearby Attractions and Landmarks

  1. Zakher Park: A green space for relaxation and outdoor activities.

  2. Al Ain Palace Museum: Dive into Al Ain’s royal history and cultural heritage.

  3. Sheikh Khalifa Bin Zayed Grand Mosque: A stunning example of Islamic architecture and a peaceful space for reflection.

  4. City Centre: Enjoy shopping and dining at this bustling commercial hub.

  5. Al Jimi Mall: A shopping and leisure complex, perfect for families and visitors.

  6. Al Ain Zoo: A world-class wildlife park offering a chance to see exotic and native species, great for families and nature lovers.

7.

  1. Al Ain Oasis: A UNESCO World Heritage Site, this lush, green oasis offers a serene escape with its traditional irrigation system and palm groves.

  2. Al Jahili Fort: A historical fort that dates back to the 19th century, showcasing the region’s heritage and offering cultural exhibitions.

10.

Journey Experience

Bus 916 from Al Mottaqein Mosque to Co-op is a truly interesting ride through the heart of Al Ain and its juxtaposition of modernity and tradition.

The bus also runs near the livelier City Centre and Al Jimi Mall, for shopping and dinning. One of the standouts was seeing Al Ain’s highest peak Jebel Hafeet looming in the distance. It’s a great way to see the city, travelling on the bus route through vibrant neighborhoods and cultural sites, and conveniently and comfortably. The scenic journey on Bus 916 is a great way to see Al Ain, whether you are stopping at local mosques, or enjoying the views.

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: May 19, 2025.

Written by Al Khaleej
